description: Canonical cross-client fixtures for Beacon. Loaded by the Go server's conformance tests and by every client (starting with beacon-client / Ruby) in CI. If a client disagrees with a fixture, the client is wrong.

fingerprint.description: Fingerprint algorithm: SHA1(exception_class + '|' + first_app_frame_path). Line numbers and method names are excluded — only the file path. Output is lowercase hex.

cases.name: same file different line produces same fingerprint
cases.notes: Cosmetic edits above the failing line must not shatter grouping across deploys. Line 42 and line 99 in the same file must hash identically.

cases.name: different class same frame → different fingerprint
no line number on frame
cases.notes: Clients that can't determine the line number must still produce a stable hash using just the path.

path_normalization.description: Client-side path normalization fallback per 06-http-api.md. Framework route templates (Rails routes, Express req.route.path, FastAPI route.path) should be preferred when available. When not available, clients MUST apply this fallback to produce a normalized perf metric name of the form '<METHOD> <normalized_path>'.

envelope.description: Example minimal valid and invalid POST /events envelopes. Clients should use these to verify their serializer and the server's validation. These are not asserted against the Go reference server in the Go conformance test — they're for client-side unit tests.

cases.name: unknown kind is rejected
cases.expected_error_contains: kind must be outcome, perf, or error

batch_flush.description: Client-side async flusher trigger rules. beacon-client and any well-behaved client MUST flush a batch when the earliest of these conditions is met.

rules.name: size trigger
rules.notes: A client at steady state should send one batch every max_batch_size events.

rules.name: age trigger
rules.notes: A low-traffic app should still see events appear within a second.

rules.name: body size safety trigger
rules.notes: Leaves headroom for the 1 MB server ceiling — flushing earlier avoids a 413 on a long-tail property.

rules.name: shutdown flush
rules.condition: process receives SIGTERM or at_exit hook fires
rules.notes: Bounded wait (5s) so shutdown doesn't hang if beacon is unreachable.

unreachable_beacon.description: Client behavior when POST /events cannot reach the server. Beacon is observability, not durable messaging — data loss on a long outage is acceptable.

rules.name: transport error retries
rules.condition: connection refused / DNS failure / read timeout
rules.behavior: exponential backoff, 5 attempts max (200ms, 400ms, 800ms, 1.6s, 3.2s); then drop the batch and log at warn

rules.name: 4xx does not retry
rules.behavior: drop the batch, log at error — the client is doing something wrong and retries will not help

rules.name: 429 honors Retry-After
rules.behavior: sleep for Retry-After seconds (default 1), then retry once; drop after the retry

rules.name: queue is bounded
rules.behavior: drop incoming events rather than grow unboundedly; increment a dropped counter the app can inspect

rules.name: circuit breaker opens after repeated failures
rules.behavior: stop attempting for 30 seconds, then try a single test post before reopening the gate

deploy_sha_propagation.description: deploy_sha flows from the client's initializer through every event's context block. The rollup worker reads it from beacon_events.context to correlate deploy baselines.

cases.name: env override wins
cases.notes: Clients should prefer env vars like GIT_SHA/KAMAL_VERSION when set so a deploy reflects the actual commit.

cases.name: unset deploy_sha is absent, not empty string
